```json
{
  "workflow_name": "HTTP Request Handling Pipeline",
  "flow": [
    "User creates a Fastify instance via fastify() in fastify.js (line ~29) and calls .listen() which uses createServer(options, httpHandler) in lib/server.js (line ~260) to create a Node.js HTTP server",
    "Node.js server emits 'request' event, calling httpHandler (wrapRouting result in fastify.js, line ~457) → preRouting checks rewriteUrl, then calls router.routing(req, res, ...) which is find-my-way's router.lookup.bind(router) in lib/route.js (line ~52)",
    "find-my-way matches URL/method to the registered route and invokes routeHandler(req, res, params, context, query) in lib/route.js (line 457)",
    "routeHandler creates request (new context.Request(...)) and reply (new context.Reply(...)) in lib/route.js (lines ~526-527), sets up handler timeout (lines ~531-546), setupResponseListeners (line ~549), and starts the hook lifecycle",
    "onRequest hook runner (line ~556): onRequestHookRunner(context.onRequest, request, reply, runPreParsing) from lib/hooks.js",
    "runPreParsing in lib/route.js (line ~617): sets request payload stream, runs preParsing hooks if any, then calls handleRequest.call(request.server, null, request, reply)",
    "handleRequest in lib/handle-request.js (line 21): determines if method is bodyless (GET/HEAD/TRACE) or bodywith (POST/PUT/etc). For bodyless methods, calls handler(request, reply) immediately. For bodywith methods, checks content-type, runs content-type parser if needed, then calls handler",
    "handler in lib/handle-request.js (line ~74): executes preValidation hooks → preValidationCallback → schema validation → validationCompleted → preHandler hooks → preHandlerCallback → preHandlerCallbackInner",
    "preHandlerCallbackInner in lib/handle-request.js (line ~97): invokes the actual user route handler context.handler(request, reply). If handler returns a thenable, wraps with wrapThenable (lib/wrap-thenable.js). If handler returns a value directly, calls reply.send(result)",
    "reply.send(payload) in lib/reply.js (line ~170): processes payload type (object/string/stream/buffer/null), runs preSerialization hooks, serializes to JSON via serialize() or custom serializer, then runs onSend hooks via onSendHookRunner",
    "onSendEnd in lib/reply.js (line ~380): writes headers via safeWriteHead, sends payload (string/buffer via writePayload, stream via sendStream, web stream via sendWebStream). For trailers, uses sendTrailer. Calls res.end() to complete",
    "setupResponseListeners (lib/reply.js, line ~783): listens on raw response 'finish'/'error' events. On finish, runs onResponse hooks via onResponseHookRunner, then logs request completion via requestCompleted",
    "If any error occurs during hooks/handler/serialization, onErrorHook (lib/reply.js, line ~770) runs onError hooks, then handleError (lib/error-handler.js, line 30) walks the error handler prototype chain, falling back to defaultErrorHandler which serializes the error via serializeError and sends it back"
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "LISTENING → REQUEST_RECEIVED: Node.js emits 'request' event on the HTTP server (lib/server.js)",
    "REQUEST_RECEIVED → ROUTED: find-my-way.lookup matches URL+method to a stored route + context (lib/route.js:52)",
    "ROUTED → HOOKS_STARTED: routeHandler creates request/reply objects and enters hook lifecycle (lib/route.js:556)",
    "HOOKS_STARTED → PARSING: onRequest hooks complete → runPreParsing sets request payload stream (lib/route.js:617–628)",
    "PARSING → VALIDATING: preParsing hooks complete → handleRequest determines content-type parsing strategy (lib/handle-request.js:21–72)",
    "VALIDATING → HANDLER_EXECUTING: preValidation hooks pass → schema validation completes → preHandler hooks pass (lib/handle-request.js:74–130)",
    "HANDLER_EXECUTING → RESPONDING: user handler returns or calls reply.send() → preSerialization hooks run → payload serialized (lib/reply.js:170–250, lib/handle-request.js:130–147)",
    "RESPONDING → COMPLETED: onSend hooks run → headers written via safeWriteHead → payload flushed via writePayload/sendStream/sendWebStream → res.end() (lib/reply.js:380–465)",
    "COMPLETED → LOGGED: response 'finish' event → onResponse hooks → requestCompleted() log (lib/reply.js:783–804)",
    "ANY STATE → ERROR: Hook or handler error → onErrorHook → handleError walks error handler chain → fallbackErrorHandler serializes error → writes error response (lib/error-handler.js:30–110, lib/reply.js:770–780)"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "Node.js HTTP Server (lib/server.js): createServer creates http/https/http2.Server instances via http.createServer/https.createServer/http2.createServer. The httpHandler callback (preRouting → router.routing) is passed as the 'request' event listener. Socket-level events (clientError, timeout, upgrade) are handled separately.",
    "find-my-way Router (lib/route.js: ~line 30): External routing library find-my-way is used for URL pattern matching, constraint evaluation, and route dispatch. Fastify calls router.on() to register routes and router.lookup() to dispatch, passing routeHandler as the matched handler.",
    "Content-Type Parsing (lib/content-type-parser.js): External content-type parsing framework. run() dispatches to registered parsers (default JSON and text parsers). Users can add custom parsers via addContentTypeParser.",
    "Schema Validation/Serialization (lib/validation.js, lib/schemas.js): Called during preValidation and preSerialization. Default uses Ajv (JSON Schema) for validation and fast-json-stringify for serialization. Users can supply custom compilers via setValidatorCompiler/setSerializerCompiler.",
    "Logging (lib/logger-factory.js, lib/logger-pino.js): Fastify wraps pino logger. Creates child loggers per request with request-id. Uses null logger when logging is disabled.",
    "Plugin System (avvio/plugin-override.js): Fastify uses avvio for plugin registration, encapsulation, and boot sequencing. Each plugin gets its own encapsulated context (hooks, decorators, schemas)."
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Server Closing (lib/route.js:473–494): If closing=true and return503OnClosing is true, all incoming requests get an immediate 503 response without running the handler chain. This is a load-shedding fast path.",
    "Bad URL (fastify.js:401–416): onBadUrl handler catches malformed URLs. If options.frameworkErrors is set, dispatches to a user-provided handler; otherwise returns a 400 JSON response directly.",
    "Invalid Content-Type (lib/handle-request.js:45–50): If content-type header is present but invalid (ContentType.from returns isValid=false), reply gets HTTP 415 with FST_ERR_CTP_INVALID_MEDIA_TYPE.",
    "Schema Validation Error (lib/handle-request.js:82–97): If schema validation fails, the error is sent via reply.send(). If attachValidation is true, the error is stored in request.validationError and the handler still runs.",
    "Handler Timeout (lib/route.js:531–547): If handlerTimeout > 0, an AbortController + setTimeout monitors handler execution. If the timeout fires before reply.sent, it sends FST_ERR_HANDLER_TIMEOUT error and aborts the request.",
    "Handler/Sync Hook Throw (lib/handle-request.js:140–147, lib/hooks.js hookRunnerGenerator): Any synchronous throw in the handler or hooks is caught in a try-catch block, sets reply[kReplyIsError]=true, and calls reply.send(err).",
    "Promise Rejection (lib/wrap-thenable.js:12–82): If the handler returns a rejecting promise, wrapThenable catches the rejection, sets reply[kReplyIsError]=true, and calls reply.send(err). Also handles reply.sent === true guard to prevent double-send.",
    "Serialization Failure (lib/reply.js:324–335, lib/error-handler.js:82–96): If serialize() or custom serializer throws, wrapSerializationError attaches route config and calls onErrorHook → handleError. Fallback uses serializeError for JSON error output.",
    "Double Send (lib/reply.js:174–178): If reply.send() is called after reply.sent is already true, it logs a warning with FST_ERR_REP_ALREADY_SENT and returns early without sending again.",
    "send() Inside onError Hook (lib/reply.js:170–172): If reply.send() is called while reply[kReplyIsRunningOnErrorHook] is true, it throws FST_ERR_SEND_INSIDE_ONERR to prevent infinite error handling loops.",
    "Error Handler Chain Exhaustion (lib/error-handler.js:35–42): If reply[kReplyNextErrorHandler] becomes false (end of prototype chain), fallbackErrorHandler serializes the error via serialize() or serializeError and writes it directly with raw.writeHead + raw.end."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "fastify.js: wrapRouting creates preRouting → router.routing = router.lookup.bind(router) → httpHandler passed to createServer",
    "fastify.js: defaultRoute (line ~385) handles unmatched routes via fourOhFour.router.lookup(req, res)",
    "lib/server.js: createServer/getServerInstance creates http/https/http2 servers with httpHandler as the request callback",
    "lib/route.js: buildRouting returns { routing, route, routeHandler, ... } — routeHandler (line 457) is the matched route entry point",
    "lib/route.js: runPreParsing (line 617) bridges hook lifecycle into lib/handle-request.js's handleRequest",
    "lib/handle-request.js: handleRequest (line 21) determines body parsing strategy and calls handler (line 74)",
    "lib/handle-request.js: handler → preValidationHookRunner → preValidationCallback (schema validation) → validationCompleted → preHandlerHookRunner → preHandlerCallback → preHandlerCallbackInner → context.handler(request, reply)",
    "lib/handle-request.js: preHandlerCallbackInner (line 97) calls user handler, uses wrapThenable for async handlers, or reply.send() for sync returns",
    "lib/reply.js: Reply.prototype.send (line 170) processes payload type, runs preSerialization hooks via preSerializationHook, runs onSend hooks via onSendHookRunner, then onSendEnd writes headers + payload",
    "lib/reply.js: onSendEnd (line 380) → safeWriteHead → writePayload/sendStream/sendWebStream → sendTrailer → res.end()",
    "lib/reply.js: setupResponseListeners (line 783) hooks into response 'finish'/'error' events for onResponse hooks and logging",
    "lib/hooks.js: onRequestHookRunner, preParsingHookRunner, preValidationHookRunner, preHandlerHookRunner, onSendHookRunner, preSerializationHookRunner, onResponseHookRunner, onTimeoutHookRunner — all lifecycle hook runners",
    "lib/error-handler.js: handleError (line 30) walks error handler prototype chain; fallbackErrorHandler (line 59) serializes and writes error response",
    "lib/wrap-thenable.js: wrapThenable (line 12) handles async handler resolution/rejection with reply.sent guards",
    "lib/error-serializer.js: serializeError for final fallback JSON error output"
  ],
  "summary": "The representative user-facing workflow is the HTTP Request Handling Pipeline. It begins when a client sends an HTTP request to a running Fastify server. The Node.js HTTP server's 'request' event fires the httpHandler callback (wrapRouting in fastify.js), which optionally rewrites the URL and dispatches to find-my-way's router.lookup(). The router matches the method+URL and calls routeHandler() in lib/route.js. routeHandler creates Request and Reply objects, sets up handler timeout and response listeners, and then runs the lifecycle hook chain: onRequest → runPreParsing (payload stream setup + preParsing hooks) → handleRequest (content-type parsing strategy) → handler (preValidation hooks → schema validation → preHandler hooks → actual route handler). After the handler executes, reply.send() processes the payload (serialization/streaming), runs preSerialization and onSend hooks, and writes headers + payload to the Node.js response via safeWriteHead/writePayload. The response 'finish' event triggers onResponse hooks and request completion logging. Errors at any stage are caught and routed through onErrorHook → handleError, which walks an encapsulated error handler prototype chain, falling back to a JSON error serializer. External boundaries include the Node.js HTTP server, find-my-way router, content-type parser framework, Ajv schema validation, pino logging, and avvio plugin system."
}
```

Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive analysis.

```json
{
  "workflow_name": "HTTP Request Handling Lifecycle (GET / route with JSON response)",
  "flow": [
    "1. TCP connection arrives at Node.js HTTP server → triggers `server.on('request', httpHandler)` where `httpHandler` is `preRouting()` defined in `fastify.js` `wrapRouting()`",
    "2. `preRouting()` checks `rewriteUrl` option, then delegates to `router.routing(req, res, cb)` — i.e. `FindMyWay.lookup()` (`lib/route.js` router object)",
    "3. `FindMyWay` matches the URL/method and calls `routeHandler(req, res, params, context, query)` (`lib/route.js` routeHandler function)",
    "4. `routeHandler` generates a request ID (`getGenReqId`), creates child logger, instantiates `new context.Request(...)` (`lib/request.js`) and `new context.Reply(...)` (`lib/reply.js`), sets up `setupResponseListeners(reply)` for timing and onResponse",
    "5. If `handlerTimeout > 0`, an `AbortController` and timeout timer are created; socket 'close' listener is added",
    "6. Runs `onRequest` hooks (if any) via `onRequestHookRunner` (`lib/hooks.js`), then continues to `runPreParsing()`",
    "7. `runPreParsing()` (`lib/route.js`) runs `preParsing` hooks (if any) against the raw request stream, then calls `handleRequest()` (`lib/handle-request.js`)",
    "8. For a GET request (bodyless method), `handleRequest()` skips body parsing and calls inner `handler()` directly",
    "9. `handler()` runs `preValidation` hooks via `preValidationHookRunner`, then calls `preValidationCallback()`",
    "10. `preValidationCallback()` calls `validateSchema(reply[kRouteContext], request)` (`lib/validation.js` validate function) — validates `params`, `body`, `query`, `headers` against compiled schemas",
    "11. If validation passes, calls `validationCompleted()` which runs `preHandler` hooks via `preHandlerHookRunner`",
    "12. `preHandlerCallback()` / `preHandlerCallbackInner()` invokes `context.handler(request, reply)` — the user's route handler function",
    "13. If handler returns a thenable (Promise), `wrapThenable()` (`lib/wrap-thenable.js`) awaits it and calls `reply.send(payload)`; if handler calls `reply.send(payload)` directly, that path is taken",
    "14. `reply.send()` (`lib/reply.js`) checks for errors (→ `onErrorHook`), determines content-type, runs `preSerialization` hooks (via `preSerializationHookRunner`), then runs `onSend` hooks (via `onSendHookRunner`)",
    "15. `onSendEnd()` writes status code/headers via `safeWriteHead()`, sets content-length, writes payload, and calls `res.end()`",
    "16. On response completion, `setupResponseListeners` callback fires: cleans up timeout resources, runs `onResponse` hooks, and calls `reply.server[kLogController].requestCompleted()`"
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "Initial: `kState = { listening: false, closing: false, started: false, ready: false, booting: false, aborted: false }` (fastify.js)",
    "After `fastify.ready()` completes: `kState.started = true` → avvio emits 'start' (fastify.js avvio 'start' handler)",
    "After `server.listen()` succeeds: `kState.listening = true` (lib/server.js listenCallback / listenPromise)",
    "After `fastify.close()` is called: `kState.closing = true`; `router.closeRoutes()` is called (fastify.js preReady → onClose handler)",
    "During closing/drain: if `return503OnClosing` and `closing === true`, incoming requests get 503 'Service Unavailable' (lib/route.js routeHandler)",
    "Per-request `reply.sent` transitions: `false` → `true` after `res.end()` or `res.writeHead` completes (lib/reply.js `sent` getter checks `raw.writableEnded`)"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "Node.js HTTP/S server instance created by `lib/server.js` `getServerInstance()` — the TCP/TLS boundary where raw `req`/`res` (Node `IncomingMessage`/`ServerResponse`) enter the framework",
    "Third-party router `find-my-way` — URL-to-handler matching is entirely delegated to this external library (`lib/route.js` buildRouting returns `FindMyWay(options)`)",
    "DNS lookup in `lib/server.js` `multipleBindings()` — when listening on 'localhost', `dns.lookup()` resolves all IP addresses to bind secondary servers",
    "Body stream — `request.raw` (Node `IncomingMessage`) is the parsed HTTP body stream; content-type parsers read it; `lib/content-type-parser.js` manages parser selection",
    "Response stream — `reply.raw` (Node `ServerResponse`) is the output boundary; payloads are piped or written to it",
    "Logger (`pino`) — configured via `options.logger`; logging happens at `kLogController` boundaries (incomingRequest, requestCompleted, routeNotFound, etc.)"
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Bad URL / invalid path → `onBadUrl` in `fastify.js` returns 400 (FST_ERR_BAD_URL)",
    "Max parameter length exceeded → `onMaxParamLength` returns 414 (FST_ERR_MAX_PARAM_LENGTH)",
    "Server closing (graceful shutdown) → `closing === true` check in `lib/route.js` routeHandler returns 503 'Service Unavailable'",
    "Unsupported Media Type → `handleRequest()` in `lib/handle-request.js` returns 415 (FST_ERR_CTP_INVALID_MEDIA_TYPE)",
    "Schema validation failure → `validateSchema()` returns error, sent as 400 (FST_ERR_VALIDATION) or schema-defined status",
    "Handler throws synchronously → caught in `preHandlerCallbackInner()` via try-catch, `reply.send(err)` is called",
    "Handler returns rejected Promise → `wrapThenable()` rejection handler calls `reply.send(err)`",
    "Handler timeout → `AbortController.abort()` fires after configured `handlerTimeout` ms; `FST_ERR_HANDLER_TIMEOUT` (503) sent",
    "Serialization failure → `preSerializationHookEnd` or `serialize()` throws → caught and routed to `onErrorHook` → `handleError()` in `lib/error-handler.js`",
    "Double-send → `reply.send()` checks `reply.sent` and logs warning with `FST_ERR_REP_ALREADY_SENT`",
    "Client abort during request → `req.on('close')` triggers `onRequestAbortHookRunner` in `routeHandler()`",
    "Plugin boot failure → `fastify.ready()` rejects with Avvio error (mapped via `AVVIO_ERRORS_MAP` in `lib/errors.js`)"
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "fastify.js:wrapRouting() — lines ~135-146: `preRouting` is the `httpHandler` passed to `createServer`",
    "fastify.js:createServer() import — `const { createServer } = require('./lib/server')`",
    "lib/server.js:getServerInstance() — creates `http.createServer(httpHandler)` or `https.createServer(...)`",
    "lib/route.js:buildRouting() — returns `{ routing: router.lookup.bind(router), route, routeHandler, ... }`",
    "lib/route.js:routeHandler() — lines ~340-465: full request setup, hooks invocation, request/reply creation",
    "lib/route.js:runPreParsing() — lines ~505-522: bridge from route.js to handle-request.js",
    "lib/handle-request.js:handleRequest() — content-type dispatch, bodyless vs bodywith method handling",
    "lib/handle-request.js:handler() → preValidationCallback() → validationCompleted() → preHandlerCallback() → preHandlerCallbackInner(): full validation+handler chain",
    "lib/handle-request.js:preHandlerCallbackInner() — line ~120: `result = context.handler(request, reply)` — the user handler invocation",
    "lib/validation.js:validate() — validates params, body, query, headers against compiled schemas",
    "lib/reply.js:Reply.prototype.send() — orchestration of onError→preSerialization→onSend→serialize→write",
    "lib/reply.js:onSendEnd() — `safeWriteHead()` then `writePayload()` then `res.end()`",
    "lib/reply.js:setupResponseListeners() — tracks start/end times, runs onResponse hook on finish",
    "lib/hooks.js:onRequestHookRunner, preParsingHookRunner, preValidationHookRunner, preHandlerHookRunner, onSendHookRunner — all lifecycle hook runners",
    "lib/context.js:Context — stores schema, handler, hooks, contentTypeParser, errorHandler per route",
    "lib/wrap-thenable.js:wrapThenable() — handles Promise-returning handlers",
    "lib/error-handler.js:handleError() → fallbackErrorHandler() — error serialization chain",
    "lib/four-oh-four.js — 404 handling with encapsulated fallback routing"
  ],
  "summary": "The most representative user-facing workflow in Fastify is the end-to-end processing of an HTTP request: from TCP connection, through find-my-way routing, lifecycle hooks (onRequest → preParsing → preValidation → preHandler), content-type parsing, schema validation, user route handler execution, response serialization, and final response write. This workflow is orchestrated across fastify.js (entry point, pre-routing), lib/route.js (routing, full request setup), lib/handle-request.js (body parsing, validation, handler dispatch), lib/reply.js (serialization, response writing), lib/hooks.js (lifecycle hook runners), lib/validation.js (schema validation), lib/context.js (per-route state), lib/error-handler.js (error handling chain), and lib/server.js (server creation/listening). Key failure points include bad URLs (400), validation errors (400/415), handler timeouts (503), serialization failures (500), and graceful-shutdown 503s."
}
```
